description: prConcurrentLimit: 2 — allows one automated PR and one unmerged major PR to coexist. A single slot would let a major PR waiting for manual merge block the entire automated queue. rebaseWhen: behind-base-branch on automated rules makes the automated slot effectively serial (each merge triggers a rebase + CI re-run on the next), so two slots do not cause package-lock.json race conditions.

schedule: before 5am on monday through friday — Renovate opens new PRs only during the midnight-to-5am window on weekdays (Europe/Zurich). platformAutomerge merges a PR the moment CI passes, so the last batch (Fri 00:00-05:00) runs CI during Friday and merges before the weekend. lockFileMaintenance keeps its own Monday schedule.

rebaseWhen: conflicted (global) — major PRs sit unmerged intentionally; rebasing them on every automated merge would waste CI runs on a PR nobody is about to merge. Automated rules override this with behind-base-branch to keep the automerge chain moving.

vulnerabilityAlerts disabled — Dependabot handles security advisories instead. Its native GitHub Security Advisory integration links CVEs to the Security tab with GHSA references and co-locates with secret scanning and code scanning, which Renovate cannot replicate. Tradeoff: Dependabot covers only direct dependencies and is slower than Renovate would be, but the GitHub-native security workflow outweighs that for this project.

packageRules.description: Add javascript label to match Dependabot language label convention
Add github-actions label to match Dependabot convention
Add docker label to match Dependabot convention
Angular and NX: manual upgrade only (ng update / nx migrate)
TypeScript: tightly coupled to Angular/NX version
ts-node: major updates disabled — each major drops older TypeScript versions; must align with frozen TypeScript
ESLint core + TypeScript ESLint: frozen until Angular/NX upgrade unlocks ESLint 9 flat config migration
ESLint plugins and Airbnb configs: major updates disabled — coupled to frozen ESLint 8; a plugin major dropping ESLint 8 support would break the entire lint pipeline
ng-packagr: coupled to Angular version
@ngrx/*: all packages are Angular-version coupled — upgrade only after ng update
@types/node: must match Node.js version used in CI and local toolchain
Node.js (.nvmrc): major version managed manually — must align with CI runners and @types/node
CKEditor: custom build + Angular adapter tracked in DEV-6208; disable both until resolved
zone.js: maintenance mode — allow patches only (disable minor and major)
NX-wrapped tools: major version ceiling is set by @nx/* peer deps — disable majors until nx migrate lifts the constraint
Angular-coupled ecosystem: major version must align with Angular major — upgrade only after ng update

Major updates: PRs open automatically but require manual merge. Timing matters — merging a major before a release or production deployment could introduce undetected breakage that poor test coverage would miss. Human decides when it is safe to merge.
SWC compiler suite — all packages must update together to avoid ABI mismatches between core, helpers, and jest transform
Grafana Faro observability SDK — packages are released together and must stay in sync
JSON-LD + its type definitions
ngx-translate i18n — core and http-loader are always used together
ESLint plugins and Airbnb configs — group patch/minor together; major is disabled above
Jest test framework and utilities — jest-preset-angular bridges Jest and Angular so it must move with the Jest group
Prettier and ESLint-prettier bridge (prettier is not in the ESLint monorepo group)
PostCSS + autoprefixer (autoprefixer not in postcss monorepo preset)
OpenSeaDragon image viewer
UUID
Test coverage tooling
Third-party Angular-compatible packages (not strictly version-coupled to Angular)

Storybook packages — @storybook/test-runner lives in a separate repo (storybookjs/test-runner) and is not covered by the monorepo:storybook preset in config:recommended; explicit group prevents sync drift on minor/patch updates
Automerge all dev dependency updates (patch + minor) — no age guard; dev deps don't affect production
Automerge production patch updates — 3-day age guard prevents adopting broken releases
Automerge production minor updates — 7-day age guard for extra caution on production code
Automerge github-actions and docker patch/minor updates — matchDepTypes: devDependencies does not apply to these managers so they need an explicit rule
